title: My favorite day hike in Yosemite: Four Mile up, Panorama down
dek: Up the Four Mile Trail to Glacier Point, down the Panorama Trail past Illilouette and Nevada Falls. A 13-mile loop that climbs 3,200 feet and gives you back the whole park. The logistics that make it work.
placeholder: Looking up the Four Mile Trail toward Glacier Point

title: Your last-minute Yosemite trip with kids: a naturalist's honest guide
dek: A senior Yosemite naturalist's honest guide to visiting with kids in 2026, no advance reservations needed. Kid-friendly hikes, timing tricks, YARTS bus tips, and what most families get wrong.
seoDek: A senior naturalist's guide to Yosemite with kids in 2026, no reservations needed. Kid-friendly hikes, timing, YARTS, and what families get wrong.

title: Tioga Road opens May 15: a plan for opening weekend
dek: Tioga Road and Tioga Pass open Friday, May 15, well ahead of the long-term average. What's open in Tuolumne Meadows, the road conditions, the short hikes that actually work in mid-May, and how to make a day of it east to Lee Vining and Mono Lake.
seoDek: Tioga Road opens May 15, 2026. What's open in Tuolumne Meadows, road conditions, short hikes for mid-May, and a day east to Lee Vining and Mono Lake.
placeholder: Tuolumne Meadows in early season, Tioga Road

faq.q: When does Tioga Road open in 2026?
faq.a: Tioga Road in Yosemite National Park opens Friday, May 15, 2026, well ahead of the long-term average of May 28.

faq.q: Is Tioga Pass open right now?
faq.a: As of May 13, 2026, Tioga Pass is plowed and scheduled to open Friday, May 15. Text 'ynptraffic' to 333111 for live updates, or check nps.gov/yose/planyourvisit/tioga.htm.

faq.q: Is Highway 120 through Yosemite open?
faq.a: Yes. Highway 120 (Tioga Road) through Yosemite opens Friday, May 15, 2026. Highway 120 west of the park is open year-round.

faq.q: What is the elevation of Tioga Pass?
faq.a: Tioga Pass sits at 9,945 feet (3,031 m), the highest state highway pass in California.

faq.q: Do I need a reservation to enter Yosemite in 2026?
faq.a: No. The day-use reservation system is not in effect in 2026. A standard Yosemite entrance pass is required.

faq.q: Is the Tuolumne Meadows store open on opening weekend?
faq.a: No. The Tuolumne Meadows Grill opens May 30, the lodge June 5, and the store opens later in summer (currently scheduled for August).

faq.q: Can I camp at Tuolumne Meadows on opening weekend?
faq.a: Yes. The newly rehabilitated Tuolumne Meadows Campground is reservable on Recreation.gov for the 2026 season.

faq.q: How long does it take to drive Tioga Road?
faq.a: Tioga Road runs about 39 miles from Crane Flat to Tioga Pass. Allow about 90 minutes one-way without stops, or a full day with Olmsted Point, Tenaya Lake, and Tuolumne Meadows.

title: So you want to hike Half Dome
dek: The honest case for the cables, and the better hike most visitors don't know about: Clouds Rest, a thousand feet higher than Half Dome, with bigger views and no permit required.
seoDek: The honest case for the Half Dome cables, and the better hike most miss: Clouds Rest, a thousand feet higher, with bigger views and no permit.
placeholder: Half Dome rising 4,800 feet from the Yosemite Valley floor

title: How the Half Dome permit lottery actually works
dek: There are two lotteries, not one. The preseason in March and the daily lottery every day the cables are up. The real odds, the strategy that works, and what to do if you don't win.
seoDek: Two Half Dome lotteries, not one: preseason in March and the daily during cables season. Real odds, the strategy that works, what to do if you lose.
placeholder: Half Dome at alpenglow from Glacier Point

faq.q: When are the Half Dome permit lotteries?
faq.a: There are two. The preseason lottery runs March 1-31 (Eastern time) with results in mid-April. The daily lottery runs every day the cables are up, with applications due by 4 PM Pacific two days before your planned hike.

faq.q: What are the real odds of getting a Half Dome permit?
faq.a: In 2024 the preseason lottery had a 22% application success rate. The daily lottery ran 19% overall — about 22% on weekdays and 14% on weekends. Late-season weekdays in September and October have the best odds of any window.

faq.q: What happens if I don't win the Half Dome lottery?
faq.a: Run the daily lottery every day of your trip. Five tries at roughly 20% odds is about a 67% chance of winning at least once. If you still don't get a permit, hike Clouds Rest instead — it's a thousand feet higher than Half Dome and requires no permit.

faq.q: Can I hike Half Dome without a permit?
faq.a: Not legally. The permit checkpoint at the base of the subdome is staffed by rangers. Hiking past it without a permit is a federal violation (36 CFR 1.6), punishable by a fine of up to $5,000 or six months in jail.

faq.q: When are the cables up on Half Dome?
faq.a: Typically the Friday before Memorial Day through the day after the second Monday in October. The Park Service announces actual dates each year based on snow, weather, and crew availability.

faq.q: How much does the Half Dome permit cost?
faq.a: $10 per application (non-refundable) plus $10 per person if awarded. Each application can cover up to six people. The per-person fee is refundable if you cancel by 11:59 PM Pacific the day before your hike date.

title: Glacier Point Road is open: a plan for the early season
dek: The road climbs seventeen miles to a viewpoint at 7,200 feet that puts you at eye level with Half Dome. What is open at the top, what is not, and how to think about the first weeks of the 2026 season.
seoDek: Seventeen miles to a 7,200-foot viewpoint at eye level with Half Dome. What is open at the top, what is not, and how to plan early-season 2026.

title: The Mist Trail: everything the internet isn't telling you
dek: The most hiked trail in any national park generates more questions than every other Yosemite trail combined. The honest answers about shoes, water, when to go, and whether you can actually die out there.
seoDek: The most hiked trail in any national park. The honest answers about shoes, water, when to go, and whether you can actually die out there.
placeholder: Nevada Fall and Liberty Cap from the John Muir Trail

faq.q: How hard is the Mist Trail?
faq.a: It depends on how far you go. The 1.6-mile round trip to the Vernal Fall footbridge is moderate. The 2.6-mile round trip to the top of Vernal Fall climbs 1,000 feet on wet granite stairs and is strenuous. The full 5.4-mile round trip to the top of Nevada Fall gains 2,000 feet and takes most people 4-6 hours.

faq.q: What shoes should I wear on the Mist Trail?
faq.a: Hiking boots or trail shoes with real tread. The granite staircase below Vernal Fall is wet for most of May and June, and slippery footwear is the cause of most Mist Trail injuries. Worn sneakers, flip-flops, and sandals are genuinely dangerous here.

faq.q: When is the Mist Trail wettest?
faq.a: Peak waterfall flow runs mid-April through late June, with the heaviest spray between the base and top of Vernal Fall in May. You'll get soaked, not sprinkled. By August the falls are reduced and the trail is mostly dry.

faq.q: Can kids hike the Mist Trail?
faq.a: Older, reasonably fit kids can do the 1.6-mile round trip to the Vernal Fall footbridge. Past the bridge, the staircase to the top of Vernal Fall is steep, wet, and exposed — not appropriate for young children. The full route to Nevada Fall is a serious workout.

faq.q: Can I die on the Mist Trail?
faq.a: People have. In 2025 roughly 30% of Yosemite's search-and-rescue operations originated on the Mist Trail corridor, and the park averages close to one fatality per month on this trail and the falls above it. Stay behind the railings and don't wade above either waterfall.

faq.q: Should I go up the Mist Trail and down the John Muir Trail?
faq.a: Yes if you're doing the full route to Nevada Fall. Up the Mist Trail for the waterfall experience, down the JMT for gentler grades that are easier on knees. Total loop is about 6.5 miles. Most experienced hikers do it this way.

title: So you want to work in Yosemite
dek: Most jobs in Yosemite aren't ranger jobs. The work is hospitality, the housing starts in a tent cabin, and the closest grocery store is an hour away. The honest version of what it's like to live here, before you sign anything.
seoDek: Most jobs in Yosemite aren't ranger jobs. The work is hospitality, housing starts in a tent cabin, the grocery store is an hour away. The honest version.

title: How water ouzels live inside a waterfall
dek: A robin-sized bird walks directly into Yosemite Falls and stays there. The water ouzel is the most specialized animal in the high country, and its presence tells you the stream is healthy.
seoDek: A robin-sized bird walks directly into Yosemite Falls and stays there. The water ouzel is the most specialized animal in the high country.
placeholder: Lower Yosemite Fall in May, peak flow

title: Why a Yosemite bear in April is more dangerous than one in August
dek: A bear emerging from a winter den has lost a third of its body weight, has a digestive system that's barely awake, and is metabolically desperate. That's why spring, not summer, is the dangerous season.
seoDek: A Yosemite bear emerging from its winter den has lost a third of its weight and is metabolically desperate. Spring, not summer, is the dangerous season.
placeholder: Black bear foraging at meadow margin

title: Yosemite's disappearing glaciers, and what they record
dek: A ranger built a cairn at the toe of Mount Lyell Glacier in 1933. That cairn is now four hundred feet from the ice. The retreat is also a record of climate, written into the High Sierra in something like real time.
seoDek: A ranger built a cairn at the toe of Mount Lyell Glacier in 1933. It's now four hundred feet from the ice. The retreat is a record of climate.
placeholder: Tuolumne Meadows, the head of Lyell Canyon

title: Why giant sequoias thrive where other trees burn
dek: The same fires that kill every other tree in the Sierra are the ones the giant sequoia depends on. Two-foot bark, embedded tannins, and a seedling ecology that fails without burning.
seoDek: The fires that kill every other Sierra tree are the ones giant sequoias depend on. Two-foot bark, embedded tannins, seedlings that need burning.

title: Hetch Hetchy: the Yosemite Valley you didn't know you skipped
dek: Same elevation. Same length. Same kind of granite. Carved by the same kind of glacier as the famous one, and still mostly empty of visitors. Why almost no one goes, and why you should.
seoDek: Same elevation, same length, same granite as Yosemite Valley. Carved by the same glaciers and still mostly empty. Why no one goes, and why you should.

title: Yosemite stargazing: where to look up, and when
dek: On a moonless August night at Olmsted Point, the Milky Way doesn't look like a thin band. It casts shadows. Where to go, when to go, and how to see the sky the way our ancestors did.
seoDek: On a moonless August night at Olmsted Point, the Milky Way casts shadows. Where to go, when to go, and how to see Yosemite the way the ancients did.

title: Yosemite for non-hikers: the park you can experience without a trail
dek: Yosemite is built for non-hikers more thoroughly than almost any park in the country. A complete visit is possible without ever putting on hiking boots. Here's how to plan one.
seoDek: Yosemite is built for non-hikers more thoroughly than almost any national park. A complete visit without ever putting on hiking boots. Plan one.

title: How to pack your car for a Yosemite trip
dek: Nobody writes about packing the car. But the car is the base camp for most Yosemite trips, and what's in it decides whether a flat tire is an inconvenience or a crisis.
seoDek: Nobody writes about packing the car. But the car is the base camp for most Yosemite trips, and what's in it decides crisis from inconvenience.

title: Yosemite gateway towns compared: Mariposa, Oakhurst, Groveland, El Portal, and Lee Vining
dek: Pick the wrong gateway town and you'll burn hours of every day on the road. Pick the right one and the rest of the trip gets easier. A side-by-side from someone who's stayed in all five.
seoDek: Pick the wrong Yosemite gateway and you'll burn hours on the road. A side-by-side of Mariposa, Oakhurst, Groveland, El Portal, Lee Vining.
placeholder: El Portal, dusk on the Merced

faq.q: Which Yosemite gateway town is closest to the Valley?
faq.a: El Portal, about 14 miles and 25-30 minutes from Yosemite Valley via Highway 140. Mariposa is next at 45 miles and 45-60 minutes. Lee Vining is closest to Tuolumne Meadows (25 minutes) but 90+ minutes from the Valley.

faq.q: Which Yosemite gateway town is cheapest?
faq.a: Groveland and Mariposa typically have the widest price range and the most budget options. El Portal lodging is priced like in-park lodging because of the location. Oakhurst has predictable chain prices. Lee Vining has limited inventory and is priced accordingly.

faq.q: Which Yosemite gateway town is best for families?
faq.a: Mariposa, by a wide margin. It has full-service grocery stores, multiple restaurants, a wide range of lodging from budget motels to inns, and a real downtown to spend an evening in. The 45-minute drive to the Valley is a fair trade for the amenities.

faq.q: Which Yosemite gateway towns have full grocery stores?
faq.a: Mariposa and Oakhurst have full grocery stores. Groveland has a smaller one. El Portal has a market, not a real grocery. Lee Vining has limited groceries. Stock up at the larger town before you head in.

faq.q: Do Yosemite gateway towns have reliable cell service?
faq.a: Patchy in all five gateways and essentially nonexistent inside the park past Crane Flat. Don't rely on real-time Google Maps once you're past the entrance station. Download offline maps before you leave the gateway.

title: Yosemite during smoke season: how to actually plan around it
dek: Smoke season in California now runs July through October. The question isn't whether your trip will overlap with it. It's whether you have a plan for when it does.
seoDek: Smoke season in California now runs July through October. The question isn't whether your Yosemite trip overlaps. It's whether you have a plan.
placeholder: Half Dome through summer haze

title: Yosemite without reservations in 2026: a real strategy for the year the cap came off
dek: The reservation system was a throttle. With it gone in 2026, the park hasn't gotten easier. It's gotten harder. Here's the real strategy.
placeholder: Cathedral Rocks rising above the Merced River, Yosemite Valley

faq.q: Do I need a reservation to enter Yosemite in 2026?
faq.a: No. The day-use vehicle reservation system is not in effect in 2026. A standard Yosemite entrance pass is required.

faq.q: When does Yosemite Valley fill up?
faq.a: Day-use parking in the Valley typically fills by 9 a.m. on summer weekends and 10-11 a.m. on weekdays. Tunnel View fills earlier, often by 7:30 a.m. on peak days. Once lots are full, you're circling.

faq.q: What time should I enter Yosemite to beat the crowds?
faq.a: Be inside the park by 6:30 a.m. on any peak day. Earlier is better. The drive in is faster, the entrance stations are quiet, and you'll be parked at your first stop while everyone else is still in line.

faq.q: What changed in Yosemite in 2026?
faq.a: The day-use entrance reservation system was dropped. The reservation requirement that throttled vehicles during peak hours in 2024 and 2025 is gone, and the park is expected to see its heaviest crowds in years as a result.

faq.q: How do I find parking in Yosemite Valley?
faq.a: Arrive before 8 a.m. for the central lots. Yosemite Village day-use parking is the most useful base. If those are full, the lot near Yosemite Valley Lodge usually has turnover by mid-morning. Don't waste time circling Curry Village.

title: If it's your first time in Yosemite, read this before you book anything
dek: The bucket list isn't the problem. The strategy is. Three things turn a Yosemite visit from “we saw the things” into one of the best weeks of your life.
placeholder: Tunnel View at first light, May

faq.q: How long should I spend in Yosemite for a first trip?
faq.a: At minimum two full days. Three to four is better — it gives you time to see Yosemite Valley properly and have one full day elsewhere (Glacier Point, Mariposa Grove, or Tioga Road). A single-day trip is possible but you'll be moving constantly.

faq.q: What month is best for visiting Yosemite for the first time?
faq.a: Late May and early June for peak waterfalls; September and October for low crowds and golden light. July and August are warm and crowded. April has waterfalls and fewer people but Tioga Road and Glacier Point Road are usually still closed.

faq.q: Do I need a reservation to enter Yosemite in 2026?
faq.a: No. The day-use entrance reservation system is not in effect in 2026. A standard Yosemite entrance pass is required.

faq.q: Where should I stay on a first Yosemite trip?
faq.a: Mariposa is the most practical first-timer choice — about 45 minutes from the Valley, a real downtown, full-service. El Portal is closer (25-30 minutes) but smaller and more expensive. In-park lodging fills six to twelve months ahead.

faq.q: How much driving is involved in a Yosemite trip?
faq.a: More than most people expect. A gateway town to the Valley is 25 to 90 minutes each way. Glacier Point Road and Tioga Road each add an hour or more in-park. Pack the car like you're going to drive a lot, because you will.
